D6 Display L4 Analyze Reference

21-Step Grayscale Tracking

a calibrator walks the grey ramp in 5% steps, measuring how far each step's tint strays from neutral — the ΔE that should stay under three

Grayscale tracking measures neutrality across 21 steps from black to white. At each step the probe reports a ΔE from the target white point; a value under ~3 is hard to see. Real panels often drift — shadows leaning blue, highlights warming — and a single RGB-gain error tilts the whole ramp.

Raise the tint error and switch the error pattern — cool shadows or warm highlights. Watch the ramp pick up colour and the ΔE curve climb above the visible-threshold line. A well-tracked display keeps every step in the band.
